title: ‘Botched’ rollout of Victoria’s Myki systems blamed on ‘incompetent’ Allan government
description: Institute of Public Affairs’ Mia Schlicht says it is “unbelievable” that the Victorian government has botched the rollout of the new Myki systems.   “At the end of the day, it’s just another failed project rolled out by a financially bankrupt government who is completely incompetent,” Ms Schlicht told Sky News host Steve Price.  “It is just unbelievable, and once again it is Victorians who are paying the price.”

title: Donald Trump’s Pearl Harbour comment labelled ‘absolutely hilarious’
description: Sky News contributor Joe Hildebrand says US President Donald Trump’s comment concerning Pearl Harbour is “absolutely hilarious”.   “I just love him … this is why you have Donald Trump, if you want someone who’s going to follow all the protocols … don’t go with Donald Trump,” Mr Hildebrand told Sky News host Steve Price.  “If you want someone who is just going to go where no man, where angels have feared to tread, no man has dared to go before … I thought this was absolutely hilarious.”

title: Chris Bowen attends climate conference amid Australian fuel shortage
description: Sky News contributor Joe Hildebrand details how Minister for Climate Change and Energy Chris Bowen has attended a climate conference in the middle of a fuel crisis.   “I felt this way when Scott Morrison left for Hawaii in the middle of the bushfire crisis,” Mr Hildebrand told Sky News host Steve Price.  “Bowen goes to a conference, which is at least work related, in the middle of an energy crisis.”

title: ‘Malinauskas will be king, but Pauline Hanson is the star’: Litmus test for One Nation
description: Sky News host Laura Jayes believes that Peter Malinauskas will win the SA election; however, all eyes will be on One Nation and the federal impact of the election.   “Malinauskas will be the king, but he’s not going to be the start tonight, that is all, One Nation and Pauline Hanson,” Ms Jayes said.   “He’ll be victorious tonight, of course, but we’ll be looking at the story underneath.  “Labor will win handsomely tonight.”

title: Early analysis of the South Australian state election
description: The early analysis of the South Australian state election results reveals potential liberal vulnerabilities in seats like Flinders, Chaffey, Hammond, Narungga, and Finniss, where strong independent and One Nation candidates are contesting.   Ngadjuri is considered One Nation's best chance to pick up a seat.   In outer suburbs like Elizabeth and Playford, the focus is on how high One Nation's primary vote can get and the preference flow from the Liberal Party.   Labor is targeting marginal seats like Colton, Morphett, and Hartley for potential gains.

title: One Nation's surprising performance and potential seat gains
description: Chief Election Analyst Tom Connell provides insights into the early election results in South Australia.   While Labor appears to be leading, there are indications of a significant swing against them, with One Nation outperforming expectations in some outer suburban and regional seats.

title: Paul Murray and Cory Bernardi discuss the 2026 South Australian election
description: Sky News host Paul Murray interviews One Nation South Australia Candidate Cory Bernardi about the South Australian election.

title: Sky News projects Labor’s Peter Malinauskas has won the South Australian election
description: Sky News Chief Election Analyst Tom Connell projects Labor’s Peter Malinauskas has won the South Australian election.

title: ‘I feel vindicated’: Pauline Hanson reflects on South Australian election
description: Sky News host Paul Murray speaks with One Nation Leader Pauline Hanson about the 2026 South Australian election.

title: Australian fuel shortage sees increase in diesel theft, claims petrol station owner
description: Liberty Ryan Fuel Station Owner Robert Cook details how increases in diesel fuel theft are impacting farmers and agricultural operators.   “If I were an earth mover or agricultural operator, I'd be very careful about where I have my fuel and the tanks were well locked, and you had a few big dogs around the place,” Mr Cook told Sky News Australia.  “It’s very easy to get rid of a couple of 44-gallon loads of fuel and all of a sudden a farmer or operator could be out of pocket by five or six thousand dollars.”

title: ‘We’re coming’: Pauline Hanson hints at Victorian seats in speech after SA election
description: One Nation Leader Pauline Hanson addresses the media after the South Australian election, warning Victoria that One Nation is coming.

title: Opposition Leader Ashton Hurn concedes defeat to Peter Malinauskas in SA election
description: South Australia Opposition Leader Ashton Hurn concedes defeat after Labor's Peter Malinauskas wins the South Australian election.

title: Petrol station owner reflects on diesel supply issues amid Australian fuel shortage
description: Liberty Ryan Fuel Station Owner Robert Cook says he has had issues with diesel supply amid Australia’s fuel shortage.   “We’ve been in the business for about 40 years,” Mr Cook told Sky News Australia.  “We had a small issue with being out of diesel last week, but that’s now been resolved.”

title: Fuel suppliers ‘restricted’ from picking up ‘normal’ amount of petroleum
description: Liberty Ryan Fuel Station Owner Robert Cook says fuel suppliers are restricted in the amount of fuel they pick up from their terminals.   “There are not the normal full-sized tank loads that we’d be normally getting in,” Mr Cook told Sky News Australia.  “Our distributors themselves have been restricted from the amount of fuel they can pick up from their terminal in Brisbane.”

title: Peter Malinauskas claims victory in 2026 South Australian election
description: South Australian Premier Peter Malinauskas claims victory in the 2026 South Australian election.
